Scripts.Description: Creates a new Active Directory user account with standard attributes including name, UPN, OU placement, and initial password.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Create AD User
# ============================================================
# Requirements: RSAT Active Directory module
# Run As: Domain Admin or Account Operator
# ============================================================

Import-Module ActiveDirectory

# --- Parameters ---
$FirstName     = 'John'
$LastName      = 'Doe'
$Username      = 'jdoe'
$Password      = ConvertTo-SecureString 'P@ssw0rd123!' -AsPlainText -Force
$OU            = 'OU=Users,OU=Corp,DC=contoso,DC=com'
$Department    = 'Information Technology'
$Title         = 'Systems Administrator'
$Manager       = 'CN=Jane Smith,OU=Users,OU=Corp,DC=contoso,DC=com'
$EmailDomain   = 'contoso.com'
$Company       = 'Contoso Ltd'
$Office        = 'New York'
$Phone         = '+1-555-0100'

# --- Derived Values ---
$DisplayName   = "$FirstName $LastName"
$UPN           = "$Username@$EmailDomain"
$Email         = "$Username@$EmailDomain"

Write-Host "Creating AD user: $DisplayName ($Username)" -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {
    New-ADUser `
        -SamAccountName   $Username `
        -UserPrincipalName $UPN `
        -Name              $DisplayName `
        -GivenName         $FirstName `
        -Surname           $LastName `
        -DisplayName       $DisplayName `
        -EmailAddress      $Email `
        -Department        $Department `
        -Title             $Title `
        -Manager           $Manager `
        -Company           $Company `
        -Office            $Office `
        -OfficePhone       $Phone `
        -Path              $OU `
        -AccountPassword   $Password `
        -Enabled           $true `
        -ChangePasswordAtLogon $true `
        -PasswordNeverExpires  $false

    Write-Host "[OK] User '$Username' created successfully in '$OU'" -ForegroundColor Green

    # Verify creation
    $NewUser = Get-ADUser -Identity $Username -Properties *
    Write-Host "`nUser Details:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host "  Display Name : $($NewUser.DisplayName)"
    Write-Host "  UPN          : $($NewUser.UserPrincipalName)"
    Write-Host "  Email        : $($NewUser.EmailAddress)"
    Write-Host "  OU           : $($NewUser.DistinguishedName)"
    Write-Host "  Enabled      : $($NewUser.Enabled)"

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] Failed to create user: $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

Scripts.Description: Reads a CSV file and bulk creates multiple Active Directory users. CSV must contain: FirstName, LastName, Username, Department, Title, OU columns.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Bulk Create AD Users from CSV
# ============================================================
# CSV Headers Required:
# FirstName, LastName, Username, Department, Title, OU, Manager
# ============================================================

Import-Module ActiveDirectory

$CsvPath      = 'C:\Temp\NewUsers.csv'
$DefaultPwd   = 'Welcome123!'
$EmailDomain  = 'contoso.com'
$LogPath      = "C:\Logs\BulkUserCreation_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss').csv"
$Results      = @()

if (-not (Test-Path $CsvPath)) {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] CSV file not found: $CsvPath" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

$Users = Import-Csv $CsvPath
Write-Host "Found $($Users.Count) users to process..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

foreach ($User in $Users) {
    $DisplayName = "$($User.FirstName) $($User.LastName)"
    $UPN         = "$($User.Username)@$EmailDomain"
    $Password    = ConvertTo-SecureString $DefaultPwd -AsPlainText -Force
    $Status      = ''
    $Error_Msg   = ''

    try {
        # Check if user already exists
        if (Get-ADUser -Filter { SamAccountName -eq $User.Username } -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
            $Status    = 'SKIPPED'
            $Error_Msg = 'User already exists'
            Write-Host "[SKIP] $DisplayName ($($User.Username)) - already exists" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        } else {
            New-ADUser `
                -SamAccountName    $User.Username `
                -UserPrincipalName $UPN `
                -Name              $DisplayName `
                -GivenName         $User.FirstName `
                -Surname           $User.LastName `
                -DisplayName       $DisplayName `
                -EmailAddress      $UPN `
                -Department        $User.Department `
                -Title             $User.Title `
                -Path              $User.OU `
                -AccountPassword   $Password `
                -Enabled           $true `
                -ChangePasswordAtLogon $true

            $Status = 'SUCCESS'
            Write-Host "[OK] Created: $DisplayName" -ForegroundColor Green
        }
    } catch {
        $Status    = 'ERROR'
        $Error_Msg = $_.Exception.Message
        Write-Host "[ERROR] $DisplayName : $Error_Msg" -ForegroundColor Red
    }

    $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
        Username    = $User.Username
        DisplayName = $DisplayName
        Status      = $Status
        Error       = $Error_Msg
        Timestamp   = (Get-Date -f 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')
    }
}

$Results | Export-Csv $LogPath -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "`nResults exported to: $LogPath" -ForegroundColor Cyan

$Success = ($Results | Where-Object Status -eq 'SUCCESS').Count
$Skipped = ($Results | Where-Object Status -eq 'SKIPPED').Count
$Errors  = ($Results | Where-Object Status -eq 'ERROR').Count
Write-Host "`nSummary: Created=$Success | Skipped=$Skipped | Errors=$Errors" -ForegroundColor Yellow

Scripts.Description: Copies an existing template user account to create a new user, inheriting group memberships and OU placement from the template.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Copy Template AD User
# ============================================================

Import-Module ActiveDirectory

$TemplateUser = 'template_it'   # SAMAccountName of template
$NewFirstName = 'Jane'
$NewLastName  = 'Smith'
$NewUsername  = 'jsmith'
$NewPassword  = ConvertTo-SecureString 'Welcome123!' -AsPlainText -Force
$EmailDomain  = 'contoso.com'

# Get template user properties
$Template = Get-ADUser $TemplateUser -Properties MemberOf, Department, Title, Company, Office, DistinguishedName
if (-not $Template) {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] Template user '$TemplateUser' not found" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

# Extract OU from template DN
$OUPath = ($Template.DistinguishedName -split ',', 2)[1]

$DisplayName = "$NewFirstName $NewLastName"
$UPN         = "$NewUsername@$EmailDomain"

Write-Host "Creating '$DisplayName' from template '$TemplateUser'..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {
    New-ADUser `
        -SamAccountName    $NewUsername `
        -UserPrincipalName $UPN `
        -Name              $DisplayName `
        -GivenName         $NewFirstName `
        -Surname           $NewLastName `
        -DisplayName       $DisplayName `
        -EmailAddress      $UPN `
        -Department        $Template.Department `
        -Title             $Template.Title `
        -Company           $Template.Company `
        -Office            $Template.Office `
        -Path              $OUPath `
        -AccountPassword   $NewPassword `
        -Enabled           $true `
        -ChangePasswordAtLogon $true

    Write-Host "[OK] User created" -ForegroundColor Green

    # Copy group memberships
    Write-Host "Copying group memberships..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
    foreach ($Group in $Template.MemberOf) {
        try {
            Add-ADGroupMember -Identity $Group -Members $NewUsername
            $GroupName = ($Group -split ',')[0] -replace 'CN=',''
            Write-Host "  [OK] Added to: $GroupName" -ForegroundColor Green
        } catch {
            Write-Host "  [WARN] Could not add to group: $Group" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        }
    }

    Write-Host "`n[DONE] User '$NewUsername' created from template '$TemplateUser'" -ForegroundColor Green

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
}

Scripts.Description: Disables an Active Directory user account and logs the action with timestamp and admin who performed the operation.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Disable AD User Account
# ============================================================

Import-Module ActiveDirectory

$Username = 'jdoe'
$Reason   = 'Employee terminated - Offboarding ticket #12345'
$Admin    = $env:USERNAME

Write-Host "Disabling user: $Username" -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {
    $User = Get-ADUser -Identity $Username -Properties Enabled, Description
    if (-not $User) {
        Write-Host "[ERROR] User '$Username' not found" -ForegroundColor Red
        exit 1
    }

    if (-not $User.Enabled) {
        Write-Host "[WARN] User '$Username' is already disabled" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    } else {
        # Disable the account
        Disable-ADAccount -Identity $Username

        # Update description with audit trail
        $NewDescription = "DISABLED: $(Get-Date -f 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm') by $Admin | Reason: $Reason"
        Set-ADUser -Identity $Username -Description $NewDescription

        Write-Host "[OK] User '$Username' has been disabled" -ForegroundColor Green
        Write-Host "     Description updated with audit info" -ForegroundColor Green
    }

    # Show current status
    $Updated = Get-ADUser -Identity $Username -Properties Enabled, Description, LastLogonDate
    Write-Host "`nAccount Status:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host "  Enabled     : $($Updated.Enabled)"
    Write-Host "  Description : $($Updated.Description)"
    Write-Host "  Last Logon  : $($Updated.LastLogonDate)"

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
}

Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Export AD User Attributes
# ============================================================

Import-Module ActiveDirectory

$SearchBase = 'OU=Users,DC=contoso,DC=com'
$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\AD_Users_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss').csv"
$Filter     = '*'  # Use 'Enabled -eq $true' to filter only active

$Properties = @(
    'SamAccountName', 'GivenName', 'Surname', 'DisplayName',
    'EmailAddress', 'UserPrincipalName', 'Department', 'Title',
    'Manager', 'Company', 'Office', 'OfficePhone', 'MobilePhone',
    'StreetAddress', 'City', 'State', 'PostalCode', 'Country',
    'Description', 'Enabled', 'LockedOut', 'PasswordNeverExpires',
    'PasswordExpired', 'PasswordLastSet', 'LastLogonDate',
    'WhenCreated', 'WhenChanged', 'DistinguishedName',
    'MemberOf', 'LogonWorkstations'
)

Write-Host "Exporting users from: $SearchBase" -ForegroundColor Cyan

$Users = Get-ADUser -Filter $Filter -SearchBase $SearchBase -Properties $Properties

Write-Host "Found $($Users.Count) users. Processing..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

$Export = $Users | Select-Object @{
    N='Username';      E={ $_.SamAccountName }},
    @{N='FirstName';   E={ $_.GivenName }},
    @{N='LastName';    E={ $_.Surname }},
    @{N='DisplayName'; E={ $_.DisplayName }},
    @{N='Email';       E={ $_.EmailAddress }},
    @{N='UPN';         E={ $_.UserPrincipalName }},
    @{N='Department';  E={ $_.Department }},
    @{N='Title';       E={ $_.Title }},
    @{N='Manager';     E={ if ($_.Manager) { (Get-ADUser $_.Manager).Name } else { '' } }},
    @{N='Enabled';     E={ $_.Enabled }},
    @{N='LockedOut';   E={ $_.LockedOut }},
    @{N='LastLogon';   E={ $_.LastLogonDate }},
    @{N='PwdLastSet';  E={ $_.PasswordLastSet }},
    @{N='PwdNeverExp'; E={ $_.PasswordNeverExpires }},
    @{N='Created';     E={ $_.WhenCreated }},
    @{N='Groups';      E={ ($_.MemberOf | ForEach-Object { ($_ -split ',')[0] -replace 'CN=','' }) -join '; ' }},
    @{N='OU';          E={ ($_.DistinguishedName -split ',', 2)[1] }}

if (-not (Test-Path (Split-Path $OutputPath))) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Split-Path $OutputPath) -Force | Out-Null
}

$Export | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation -Encoding UTF8
Write-Host "[OK] Exported $($Export.Count) users to: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

Scripts.Description: Exports all members of a specified AD group, or exports all groups and their members, to a CSV report.
Generates a report of AD user accounts that have not logged in within a specified number of days.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Inactive AD Users Report
# ============================================================

Import-Module ActiveDirectory

$InactiveDays = 90
$SearchBase   = 'DC=contoso,DC=com'
$OutputPath   = "C:\Reports\InactiveUsers_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
$Cutoff       = (Get-Date).AddDays(-$InactiveDays)

Write-Host "Searching for users inactive since: $($Cutoff.ToString('yyyy-MM-dd'))" -ForegroundColor Cyan

$InactiveUsers = Get-ADUser -Filter {
    (LastLogonDate -lt $Cutoff -or -not (LastLogonDate -like '*')) -and
    Enabled -eq $true
} -SearchBase $SearchBase -Properties LastLogonDate, Department, Title, Manager, WhenCreated, EmailAddress |
Where-Object { $_.LastLogonDate -lt $Cutoff -or $null -eq $_.LastLogonDate }

Write-Host "Found $($InactiveUsers.Count) inactive users" -ForegroundColor Yellow

$Report = $InactiveUsers | Select-Object @{
    N='Username';     E={ $_.SamAccountName }},
    @{N='DisplayName'; E={ $_.DisplayName }},
    @{N='Department';  E={ $_.Department }},
    @{N='Title';       E={ $_.Title }},
    @{N='Email';       E={ $_.EmailAddress }},
    @{N='LastLogon';   E={ $_.LastLogonDate }},
    @{N='DaysSinceLogon'; E={
        if ($_.LastLogonDate) { (New-TimeSpan -Start $_.LastLogonDate -End (Get-Date)).Days }
        else { 'Never' }
    }},
    @{N='AccountCreated'; E={ $_.WhenCreated }},
    @{N='DistinguishedName'; E={ $_.DistinguishedName }}

$Report | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation

# Console summary
Write-Host "`n=== Inactive Users Report ==="
Write-Host "Threshold  : $InactiveDays days"
Write-Host "Found      : $($InactiveUsers.Count) users"
Write-Host "Report     : $OutputPath"

# Department breakdown
Write-Host "`nBy Department:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
$Report | Group-Object Department | Sort-Object Count -Descending | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "  $($_.Name): $($_.Count) users"
}

Scripts.Description: Finds and reports all currently locked-out Active Directory accounts with details about when and how many bad attempts occurred.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Locked-Out Users Report
# ============================================================

Import-Module ActiveDirectory

$SearchBase = 'DC=contoso,DC=com'
$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\LockedUsers_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd_HHmm').csv"

Write-Host "Searching for locked accounts..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

$LockedUsers = Search-ADAccount -LockedOut -UsersOnly -SearchBase $SearchBase |
Get-ADUser -Properties LockedOut, BadLogonCount, BadPasswordTime, LastBadPasswordAttempt,
    LastLogonDate, Department, Title, EmailAddress, Manager

Write-Host "Found $($LockedUsers.Count) locked account(s)" -ForegroundColor Yellow

if ($LockedUsers.Count -gt 0) {
    $Report = $LockedUsers | Select-Object @{
        N='Username';      E={ $_.SamAccountName }},
        @{N='DisplayName'; E={ $_.DisplayName }},
        @{N='Email';       E={ $_.EmailAddress }},
        @{N='Department';  E={ $_.Department }},
        @{N='Title';       E={ $_.Title }},
        @{N='Locked';      E={ $_.LockedOut }},
        @{N='BadAttempts'; E={ $_.BadLogonCount }},
        @{N='LastBadPwd';  E={ $_.LastBadPasswordAttempt }},
        @{N='LastLogon';   E={ $_.LastLogonDate }}

    $Report | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
    Write-Host "[OK] Report saved: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

    Write-Host "`nLocked Accounts:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $Report | ForEach-Object {
        Write-Host "  $($_.Username) | Bad Attempts: $($_.BadAttempts) | Last Bad: $($_.LastBadPwd)"
    }
} else {
    Write-Host "[INFO] No locked accounts found" -ForegroundColor Green
}

Scripts.Description: Performs a comprehensive Active Directory health check including replication status, FSMO roles, and domain controller status.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Active Directory Health Check Summary
# ============================================================

Import-Module ActiveDirectory

$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\ADHealthCheck_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd_HHmm').txt"
$Report     = @()

function Write-Report($Text, $Color = 'White') {
    Write-Host $Text -ForegroundColor $Color
    $Script:Report += $Text
}

Write-Report "====================================="
Write-Report " AD HEALTH CHECK REPORT"
Write-Report " Generated: $(Get-Date -f 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')"
Write-Report "====================================="

# Domain Info
Write-Report "`n[1] DOMAIN INFORMATION" -Color Cyan
$Domain = Get-ADDomain
Write-Report "  Domain        : $($Domain.DNSRoot)"
Write-Report "  Forest        : $($Domain.Forest)"
Write-Report "  DomainMode    : $($Domain.DomainMode)"
Write-Report "  PDCEmulator   : $($Domain.PDCEmulator)"
Write-Report "  RIDMaster     : $($Domain.RIDMaster)"
Write-Report "  Infrastructure: $($Domain.InfrastructureMaster)"

# FSMO Roles
Write-Report "`n[2] FSMO ROLES" -Color Cyan
$Forest = Get-ADForest
Write-Report "  Schema Master     : $($Forest.SchemaMaster)"
Write-Report "  Domain Naming     : $($Forest.DomainNamingMaster)"
Write-Report "  PDC Emulator      : $($Domain.PDCEmulator)"
Write-Report "  RID Master        : $($Domain.RIDMaster)"
Write-Report "  Infrastructure    : $($Domain.InfrastructureMaster)"

# Domain Controllers
Write-Report "`n[3] DOMAIN CONTROLLERS" -Color Cyan
$DCs = Get-ADDomainController -Filter *
foreach ($DC in $DCs) {
    $Ping = Test-Connection -ComputerName $DC.HostName -Count 1 -Quiet
    $Status = if ($Ping) { '[ONLINE]' } else { '[OFFLINE]' }
    $Color  = if ($Ping) { 'Green' } else { 'Red' }
    Write-Report "  $Status $($DC.HostName) | Site: $($DC.Site) | GC: $($DC.IsGlobalCatalog)" -Color $Color
}

# Replication Status
Write-Report "`n[4] REPLICATION STATUS" -Color Cyan
try {
    $ReplSummary = repadmin /replsummary 2>&1
    $ReplSummary | Select-Object -First 20 | ForEach-Object { Write-Report "  $_" }
} catch {
    Write-Report "  [WARN] Could not retrieve replication summary" -Color Yellow
}

# User/Computer Stats
Write-Report "`n[5] DIRECTORY STATISTICS" -Color Cyan
$EnabledUsers   = (Get-ADUser -Filter { Enabled -eq $true }).Count
$DisabledUsers  = (Get-ADUser -Filter { Enabled -eq $false }).Count
$Computers      = (Get-ADComputer -Filter *).Count
$Groups         = (Get-ADGroup -Filter *).Count
Write-Report "  Enabled Users  : $EnabledUsers"
Write-Report "  Disabled Users : $DisabledUsers"
Write-Report "  Computers      : $Computers"
Write-Report "  Groups         : $Groups"

$Report | Out-File $OutputPath -Encoding UTF8
Write-Report "`n[DONE] Report saved: $OutputPath" -Color Green

Exports Windows Event Log entries to a CSV file, with filtering by log name, event ID, and time range.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Export Windows Event Logs
# ============================================================

$ComputerName = $env:COMPUTERNAME
$LogName      = 'Security'    # System | Application | Security
$HoursBack    = 24
$MaxEvents    = 1000
$OutputPath   = "C:\Reports\EventLogs_${LogName}_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd_HH').csv"

# Optional event ID filter (leave empty for all)
$EventIDs = @(4624, 4625, 4634, 4648, 4720, 4722, 4723, 4724, 4726, 4740)
# 4624=Logon, 4625=Failed Logon, 4720=User Created, 4726=User Deleted, 4740=Lockout

$StartTime = (Get-Date).AddHours(-$HoursBack)

Write-Host "Exporting '$LogName' log from '$ComputerName' for last $HoursBack hours..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {
    $FilterHash = @{
        LogName   = $LogName
        StartTime = $StartTime
    }
    if ($EventIDs.Count -gt 0) { $FilterHash['Id'] = $EventIDs }

    $Events = Get-WinEvent -ComputerName $ComputerName -FilterHashtable $FilterHash `
                -MaxEvents $MaxEvents -ErrorAction Stop

    $Report = $Events | Select-Object `
        TimeCreated,
        Id,
        LevelDisplayName,
        @{N='Source'; E={ $_.ProviderName }},
        @{N='Message'; E={ $_.Message -replace '\r?\n', ' ' | Select-Object -First 200 }}

    $Report | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
    Write-Host "[OK] Exported $($Events.Count) events to: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

    # Summary
    Write-Host "`nEvent ID Summary:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $Events | Group-Object Id | Sort-Object Count -Descending | Select-Object -First 10 |
        ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  ID $($_.Name): $($_.Count) events" }

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
}

Scripts.Description: Enumerates all installed software on local or remote computers from the registry and exports to CSV.
Checks local or remote computers for pending reboot conditions from Windows Update, SCCM, CBS, and component stores.
Collects comprehensive system information from remote computers including OS, hardware, uptime, and installed roles.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Remote System Information
# ============================================================

$Computers  = @('SERVER01', 'SERVER02', $env:COMPUTERNAME)
$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\SystemInfo_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
$Results    = @()

foreach ($Computer in $Computers) {
    Write-Host "Querying: $Computer" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    try {
        $Info = Invoke-Command -ComputerName $Computer -ErrorAction Stop -ScriptBlock {
            $OS   = Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem
            $CPU  = Get-CimInstance Win32_Processor | Select-Object -First 1
            $BIOS = Get-CimInstance Win32_BIOS
            $Disk = Get-CimInstance Win32_LogicalDisk -Filter "DeviceID='C:'"
            $Net  = Get-CimInstance Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration | Where-Object IPEnabled

            [PSCustomObject]@{
                Hostname       = $env:COMPUTERNAME
                OS             = $OS.Caption
                OSVersion      = $OS.Version
                OSBuild        = $OS.BuildNumber
                Architecture   = $OS.OSArchitecture
                LastBoot       = $OS.LastBootUpTime
                Uptime_Days    = [math]::Round(((Get-Date) - $OS.LastBootUpTime).TotalDays, 1)
                CPU            = $CPU.Name
                CPU_Cores      = $CPU.NumberOfCores
                RAM_GB         = [math]::Round($OS.TotalVisibleMemorySize / 1MB, 2)
                RAM_Free_GB    = [math]::Round($OS.FreePhysicalMemory / 1MB, 2)
                Disk_C_GB      = [math]::Round($Disk.Size / 1GB, 2)
                Disk_C_Free_GB = [math]::Round($Disk.FreeSpace / 1GB, 2)
                IP_Address     = ($Net | Select-Object -First 1).IPAddress[0]
                Domain         = (Get-CimInstance Win32_ComputerSystem).Domain
                Manufacturer   = (Get-CimInstance Win32_ComputerSystem).Manufacturer
                Model          = (Get-CimInstance Win32_ComputerSystem).Model
                SerialNumber   = $BIOS.SerialNumber
            }
        }
        $Results += $Info
        Write-Host "  [OK] OS: $($Info.OS) | Uptime: $($Info.Uptime_Days)d | RAM: $($Info.RAM_GB)GB" -ForegroundColor Green
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  [ERROR] $Computer - $_" -ForegroundColor Red
        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{ Hostname=$Computer; OS='ERROR'; Error=$_.Exception.Message }
    }
}

$Results | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "`n[DONE] Report saved: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

Scripts.Description: Displays detailed IP configuration for all network adapters, including IP addresses, subnet masks, gateways, and DNS servers.

Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Set Static IP Address
# ============================================================
# WARNING: Run with administrator privileges
# WARNING: Verify adapter name before execution
# ============================================================

$AdapterName  = 'Ethernet'        # Run: Get-NetAdapter to find name
$IPAddress    = '192.168.1.100'
$PrefixLength = 24                # 24 = /24 = 255.255.255.0
$Gateway      = '192.168.1.1'
$DNS1         = '192.168.1.10'   # Primary DNS (e.g., DC1)
$DNS2         = '192.168.1.11'   # Secondary DNS (e.g., DC2)
$DNS3         = '8.8.8.8'        # Tertiary DNS (Google)

Write-Host "Configuring static IP on adapter: $AdapterName" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  IP      : $IPAddress/$PrefixLength"
Write-Host "  Gateway : $Gateway"
Write-Host "  DNS     : $DNS1, $DNS2"

# Check adapter exists
$Adapter = Get-NetAdapter -Name $AdapterName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if (-not $Adapter) {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] Adapter '$AdapterName' not found. Available adapters:" -ForegroundColor Red
    Get-NetAdapter | Format-Table Name, Status, LinkSpeed
    exit 1
}

try {
    # Remove existing IP configuration
    $ExistingIP = Get-NetIPAddress -InterfaceAlias $AdapterName -AddressFamily IPv4 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($ExistingIP) {
        Remove-NetIPAddress -InterfaceAlias $AdapterName -AddressFamily IPv4 -Confirm:$false
    }
    $ExistingGW = Get-NetRoute -InterfaceAlias $AdapterName -DestinationPrefix '0.0.0.0/0' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($ExistingGW) {
        Remove-NetRoute -InterfaceAlias $AdapterName -DestinationPrefix '0.0.0.0/0' -Confirm:$false
    }

    # Set new static IP
    New-NetIPAddress `
        -InterfaceAlias $AdapterName `
        -IPAddress      $IPAddress `
        -PrefixLength   $PrefixLength `
        -DefaultGateway $Gateway

    # Set DNS servers
    Set-DnsClientServerAddress `
        -InterfaceAlias $AdapterName `
        -ServerAddresses ($DNS1, $DNS2, $DNS3)

    Write-Host "`n[OK] Static IP configured successfully" -ForegroundColor Green

    # Verify
    $Config = Get-NetIPAddress -InterfaceAlias $AdapterName -AddressFamily IPv4
    Write-Host "  Configured IP: $($Config.IPAddress)/$($Config.PrefixLength)" -ForegroundColor Green

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
}

# ============================================================
# Assign Microsoft 365 Licenses
# ============================================================

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'User.ReadWrite.All', 'Organization.Read.All'

$UserUPN    = 'jdoe@contoso.com'
$SkuId      = ''   # Will be populated from available SKUs

# List available licenses first
Write-Host "Available Licenses in tenant:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
$SKUs = Get-MgSubscribedSku
$SKUs | ForEach-Object {
    $Available = $_.PrepaidUnits.Enabled - $_.ConsumedUnits
    Write-Host "  $($_.SkuPartNumber) | Available: $Available | ID: $($_.SkuId)"
}

# Assign E3 license (Microsoft 365 E3)
$LicenseSKU = $SKUs | Where-Object { $_.SkuPartNumber -eq 'SPE_E3' } | Select-Object -First 1
if (-not $LicenseSKU) {
    Write-Host "[WARN] SPE_E3 (M365 E3) not found. Using first available license..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $LicenseSKU = $SKUs | Select-Object -First 1
}

Write-Host "`nAssigning '$($LicenseSKU.SkuPartNumber)' to: $UserUPN" -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {

        -AddLicenses @(@{ SkuId = $LicenseSKU.SkuId }) `
        -RemoveLicenses @()

    Write-Host "[OK] License assigned successfully" -ForegroundColor Green

    # Verify
    $User     = Get-MgUser -UserId $UserUPN -Property AssignedLicenses, DisplayName
    $Licenses = $User.AssignedLicenses | ForEach-Object {
        ($SKUs | Where-Object SkuId -eq $_.SkuId).SkuPartNumber
    }

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red

# ============================================================
# Export M365 / Azure AD Sign-In Logs
# ============================================================

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All', 'Directory.Read.All'

$DaysBack   = 7
$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\SignInLogs_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
$StartDate  = (Get-Date).AddDays(-$DaysBack).ToString('yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ')

# Optional filter by user
$FilterUser = ''  # e.g. 'jdoe@contoso.com' or leave blank for all

Write-Host "Fetching sign-in logs for last $DaysBack days..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {
    $Filter = "createdDateTime ge $StartDate"
    if ($FilterUser) { $Filter += " and userPrincipalName eq '$FilterUser'" }

    $Logs = Get-MgAuditLogSignIn -Filter $Filter -All
    Write-Host "Retrieved $($Logs.Count) sign-in events" -ForegroundColor Yellow

    $Report = $Logs | Select-Object `
        @{N='UserPrincipalName'; E={ $_.UserPrincipalName }},
        @{N='DisplayName';      E={ $_.UserDisplayName }},
        @{N='AppName';          E={ $_.AppDisplayName }},
        @{N='SignInTime';       E={ $_.CreatedDateTime }},
        @{N='IPAddress';        E={ $_.IpAddress }},
        @{N='Location';         E={ "$($_.Location.City), $($_.Location.CountryOrRegion)" }},
        @{N='Status';           E={ $_.Status.ErrorCode }},
        @{N='FailureReason';    E={ $_.Status.FailureReason }},
        @{N='DeviceOS';         E={ $_.DeviceDetail.OperatingSystem }},
        @{N='DeviceBrowser';    E={ $_.DeviceDetail.Browser }},
        @{N='IsInteractive';    E={ $_.IsInteractive }},
        @{N='ConditionalAccess';E={ $_.ConditionalAccessStatus }},
        @{N='MFADetail';        E={ $_.MfaDetail.AuthMethod }}

    $Report | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation -Encoding UTF8
    Write-Host "[OK] Sign-in logs exported: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

    # Summary
    $Failed = ($Report | Where-Object { $_.Status -ne 0 }).Count
    Write-Host "`nSummary: Total=$($Report.Count) | Failed=$Failed | Unique Users=$($Report | Select-Object -Unique UserPrincipalName | Measure-Object).Count"

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
}

Scripts.Description: Creates an Exchange Online shared mailbox and assigns full access and send-as permissions to specified users.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Create Exchange Online Shared Mailbox
# ============================================================
# Requirements: ExchangeOnlineManagement module
# Install: Install-Module ExchangeOnlineManagement
# ============================================================

Connect-ExchangeOnline -UserPrincipalName 'admin@contoso.com'

$MailboxName    = 'IT Support'
$MailboxAlias   = 'itsupport'
$MailboxEmail   = 'itsupport@contoso.com'
$FullAccessUsers = @('jdoe@contoso.com', 'bsmith@contoso.com')
$SendAsUsers     = @('jdoe@contoso.com')

try {
    Write-Host "Creating shared mailbox: $MailboxEmail" -ForegroundColor Cyan

    New-Mailbox `
        -Shared `
        -Name          $MailboxName `
        -Alias         $MailboxAlias `
        -PrimarySmtpAddress $MailboxEmail

    Write-Host "[OK] Shared mailbox created" -ForegroundColor Green

    # Assign Full Access
    foreach ($User in $FullAccessUsers) {
        Add-MailboxPermission `
            -Identity     $MailboxEmail `
            -User         $User `
            -AccessRights FullAccess `
            -InheritanceType All `
            -AutoMapping  $true
        Write-Host "  [OK] Full Access granted to: $User" -ForegroundColor Green
    }

    # Assign Send-As
    foreach ($User in $SendAsUsers) {
        Add-RecipientPermission `
            -Identity    $MailboxEmail `
            -Trustee     $User `
            -AccessRights SendAs `
            -Confirm:$false
        Write-Host "  [OK] Send-As granted to: $User" -ForegroundColor Green
    }

    Write-Host "`n[DONE] Shared mailbox '$MailboxEmail' fully configured" -ForegroundColor Green

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
} finally {
    Disconnect-ExchangeOnline -Confirm:$false
}

System Information Write-Host "`n[1] Collecting System Information..." -ForegroundColor Yellow $OS = Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem $CPU = Get-CimInstance Win32_Processor | Select-Object -First 1 $BIOS = Get-CimInstance Win32_BIOS $CS = Get-CimInstance Win32_ComputerSystem $Disks = Get-CimInstance Win32_LogicalDisk -Filter 'DriveType=3' $Net = Get-CimInstance Win32_NetworkAdapterConfiguration | Where-Object IPEnabled $SysInfo = [PSCustomObject]@{ Hostname = $Computer Domain = $Domain OS = $OS.Caption OSVersion = $OS.Version OSBuild = $OS.BuildNumber LastBoot = $OS.LastBootUpTime Uptime = "$([math]::Round(((Get-Date) - $OS.LastBootUpTime).TotalDays,1)) days" Manufacturer = $CS.Manufacturer Model = $CS.Model SerialNumber = $BIOS.SerialNumber CPU = $CPU.Name Cores = $CPU.NumberOfCores RAM_GB = [math]::Round($CS.TotalPhysicalMemory / 1GB, 2) BIOSVersion = $BIOS.SMBIOSBIOSVersion GeneratedBy = $env:USERNAME GeneratedAt = (Get-Date -f 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss') } $SysInfo | Export-Csv "$OutputDir\SystemInfo.csv" -NoTypeInformation Write-Host " [OK] System info collected" -ForegroundColor Green #endregion #region 2. Creates a DNS A record in a specified DNS zone on a Windows DNS server.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Add DNS A Record
# ============================================================
# Requirements: DNS Server role or RSAT DNS tools
# ============================================================

$DNSServer  = 'DC01'           # Your DNS server
$ZoneName   = 'contoso.com'    # DNS zone name
$RecordName = 'webserver01'    # Hostname (without domain)
$IPAddress  = '192.168.1.150'  # Target IP address
$TTL        = 3600             # TTL in seconds (1 hour)

Write-Host "Adding DNS A Record:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  $RecordName.$ZoneName -> $IPAddress"

try {
    # Check if record already exists
    $Existing = Get-DnsServerResourceRecord -ZoneName $ZoneName -ComputerName $DNSServer `
        -Name $RecordName -RRType A -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

    if ($Existing) {
        Write-Host "[WARN] Record already exists: $($Existing.RecordData.IPv4Address)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        $Overwrite = Read-Host 'Overwrite existing record? [y/N]'
        if ($Overwrite -ne 'y') { exit }
        Remove-DnsServerResourceRecord -ZoneName $ZoneName -ComputerName $DNSServer `
            -Name $RecordName -RRType A -Force
    }

    Add-DnsServerResourceRecordA `
        -ZoneName     $ZoneName `
        -ComputerName $DNSServer `
        -Name         $RecordName `
        -IPv4Address  $IPAddress `
        -TimeToLive   (New-TimeSpan -Seconds $TTL)

    Write-Host "[OK] A record added successfully" -ForegroundColor Green

    # Verify
    $Record = Get-DnsServerResourceRecord -ZoneName $ZoneName -ComputerName $DNSServer -Name $RecordName -RRType A
    Write-Host "  Name : $($Record.HostName).$ZoneName"
    Write-Host "  IP   : $($Record.RecordData.IPv4Address)"
    Write-Host "  TTL  : $($Record.TimeToLive)"

    # Test resolution
    $Resolve = Resolve-DnsName "$RecordName.$ZoneName" -Server $DNSServer -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($Resolve) {
        Write-Host "  [OK] DNS resolves to: $($Resolve.IPAddress)" -ForegroundColor Green
    }

} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
}

# ============================================================
# RBAC Role Mapping Report
# ============================================================

$SearchBase  = 'DC=contoso,DC=com'
$OutputPath  = "C:\Reports\RBAC_Matrix_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
$RolePrefix  = 'ROLE-'   # Prefix that identifies role groups

}

$Matrix | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation

# Role summary

Scripts.Description: Identifies and optionally disables stale user and computer accounts based on last logon date, moving them to a review OU.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Stale Account Cleanup Workflow
# ============================================================
Import-Module ActiveDirectory

$InactiveDays  = 90
$ReviewOU      = 'OU=Review-Pending,DC=contoso,DC=com'
$SearchBase    = 'DC=contoso,DC=com'
$WhatIfMode    = $true    # Set $false to actually make changes
$OutputPath    = "C:\Reports\StaleCleanup_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
$Cutoff        = (Get-Date).AddDays(-$InactiveDays)
$Results       = @()

Write-Host "Stale Account Cleanup (WhatIf=$WhatIfMode)" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Threshold: $InactiveDays days (before $(($Cutoff).ToString('yyyy-MM-dd')))" -ForegroundColor Yellow

# --- Stale Users ---
Write-Host "
[1] Scanning stale user accounts..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
$StaleUsers = Get-ADUser -Filter { Enabled -eq $true } -SearchBase $SearchBase `
    -Properties LastLogonDate, Department, Description | 
    Where-Object { $null -eq $_.LastLogonDate -or $_.LastLogonDate -lt $Cutoff }

foreach ($User in $StaleUsers) {
    $Action = 'Would Disable + Move'
    if (-not $WhatIfMode) {
        Disable-ADAccount -Identity $User.SamAccountName
        Move-ADObject -Identity $User.DistinguishedName -TargetPath $ReviewOU
        $Action = 'Disabled + Moved'
    }
    Write-Host "  [$Action] $($User.SamAccountName) | Last logon: $($User.LastLogonDate)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
        Type       = 'User'
        Name       = $User.SamAccountName
        LastLogon  = $User.LastLogonDate
        Action     = $Action
        WhatIf     = $WhatIfMode
    }
}

# --- Stale Computers ---
Write-Host "
[2] Scanning stale computer accounts..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
$StaleComputers = Get-ADComputer -Filter { Enabled -eq $true } -SearchBase $SearchBase `
    -Properties LastLogonDate | 
    Where-Object { $null -eq $_.LastLogonDate -or $_.LastLogonDate -lt $Cutoff }

foreach ($Computer in $StaleComputers) {
    $Action = 'Would Disable'
    if (-not $WhatIfMode) {
        Disable-ADAccount -Identity $Computer.SamAccountName
        $Action = 'Disabled'
    }
    Write-Host "  [$Action] $($Computer.Name) | Last logon: $($Computer.LastLogonDate)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
        Type      = 'Computer'
        Name      = $Computer.Name
        LastLogon = $Computer.LastLogonDate
        Action    = $Action
        WhatIf    = $WhatIfMode
    }
}

$Results | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "
Found: $($StaleUsers.Count) stale users, $($StaleComputers.Count) stale computers"
Write-Host "Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green
if ($WhatIfMode) { Write-Host '[NOTE] WhatIfMode=$true - no changes were made. Set $false to apply.' -ForegroundColor Cyan }

Scripts.Description: Queries multiple endpoints for Windows Update status, pending updates, and last scan time, generating a compliance dashboard.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Windows Update Compliance Report
# ============================================================
$Computers  = @('SERVER01','SERVER02','WORKSTATION01',$env:COMPUTERNAME)
$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\WUCompliance_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
$Results    = @()

foreach ($Computer in $Computers) {
    Write-Host "Checking: $Computer" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    try {
        $Data = Invoke-Command -ComputerName $Computer -ErrorAction Stop -ScriptBlock {
            $UpdateSession  = New-Object -ComObject Microsoft.Update.Session
            $UpdateSearcher = $UpdateSession.CreateUpdateSearcher()
            $SearchResult   = $UpdateSearcher.Search('IsInstalled=0 and Type=Software')

            $Critical = ($SearchResult.Updates | Where-Object { $_.MsrcSeverity -eq 'Critical' }).Count
            $Important = ($SearchResult.Updates | Where-Object { $_.MsrcSeverity -eq 'Important' }).Count
            $Other    = $SearchResult.Updates.Count - $Critical - $Important

            # Last installed update
            $Searcher2   = $UpdateSession.CreateUpdateSearcher()
            $History     = $Searcher2.QueryHistory(0,$Searcher2.GetTotalHistoryCount())
            $LastInstall = ($History | Where-Object { $_.ResultCode -eq 2 } | Sort-Object Date -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).Date

            [PSCustomObject]@{
                PendingTotal    = $SearchResult.Updates.Count
                CriticalCount   = $Critical
                ImportantCount  = $Important
                OtherCount      = $Other
                LastInstallDate = $LastInstall
                OSCaption       = (Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem).Caption
                OSBuild         = (Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem).BuildNumber
            }
        }

        $Status = if ($Data.CriticalCount -gt 0) { 'CRITICAL' } elseif ($Data.PendingTotal -gt 0) { 'OUTDATED' } else { 'COMPLIANT' }
        $Color  = switch ($Status) { 'CRITICAL' { 'Red' } 'OUTDATED' { 'Yellow' } default { 'Green' } }
        Write-Host "  [$Status] Pending: $($Data.PendingTotal) (Critical: $($Data.CriticalCount))" -ForegroundColor $Color

        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
            Computer        = $Computer
            Status          = $Status
            OS              = $Data.OSCaption
            Build           = $Data.OSBuild
            PendingTotal    = $Data.PendingTotal
            CriticalUpdates = $Data.CriticalCount
            ImportantUpdates = $Data.ImportantCount
            LastInstallDate = $Data.LastInstallDate
            CheckTime       = (Get-Date -f 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm')
        }
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  [ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{ Computer=$Computer; Status='UNREACHABLE'; Error=$_.Exception.Message }
    }
}

$Results | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "
Compliant: $(($Results|Where-Object Status -eq 'COMPLIANT').Count) | Outdated: $(($Results|Where-Object Status -eq 'OUTDATED').Count) | Critical: $(($Results|Where-Object Status -eq 'CRITICAL').Count)"
Write-Host "Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

Audits all Azure Storage accounts for public access, HTTPS enforcement, TLS version, soft delete, and diagnostic settings.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Azure Storage Account Security Audit
# ============================================================
Connect-AzAccount
$SubscriptionId = 'YOUR-SUBSCRIPTION-ID'
Set-AzContext -SubscriptionId $SubscriptionId

$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\StorageAudit_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"

Write-Host 'Auditing storage accounts...' -ForegroundColor Cyan
$StorageAccounts = Get-AzStorageAccount
Write-Host "Found $($StorageAccounts.Count) storage accounts" -ForegroundColor Yellow

$Report = $StorageAccounts | ForEach-Object {
    $SA = $_
    $Key = Get-AzStorageAccountKey -ResourceGroupName $SA.ResourceGroupName -Name $SA.StorageAccountName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        Name                  = $SA.StorageAccountName
        ResourceGroup         = $SA.ResourceGroupName
        Location              = $SA.PrimaryLocation
        SKU                   = $SA.Sku.Name
        Kind                  = $SA.Kind
        HttpsOnly             = $SA.EnableHttpsTrafficOnly
        TLSVersion            = $SA.MinimumTlsVersion
        AllowBlobPublicAccess = $SA.AllowBlobPublicAccess
        PublicNetworkAccess   = $SA.PublicNetworkAccess
        SoftDeleteEnabled     = $SA.BlobServiceProperties.DeleteRetentionPolicy.Enabled
        SoftDeleteDays        = $SA.BlobServiceProperties.DeleteRetentionPolicy.Days
        VersioningEnabled     = $SA.BlobServiceProperties.IsVersioningEnabled
        AllowSharedKey        = $SA.AllowSharedKeyAccess
        InfraEncryption       = $SA.RequireInfrastructureEncryption
        CreatedAt             = $SA.CreationTime
        Tags                  = ($SA.Tags.GetEnumerator() | ForEach-Object { "$($_.Key)=$($_.Value)" }) -join '; '
        RiskyFlags            = @(
            if (-not $SA.EnableHttpsTrafficOnly)   { 'HTTP_ALLOWED' }
            if ($SA.AllowBlobPublicAccess)          { 'PUBLIC_BLOB_ACCESS' }
            if ($SA.MinimumTlsVersion -lt 'TLS1_2') { 'OLD_TLS' }
            if (-not $SA.BlobServiceProperties.DeleteRetentionPolicy.Enabled) { 'NO_SOFT_DELETE' }
        ) -join '; '
    }
}

$Report | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation

$Risky = $Report | Where-Object { $_.RiskyFlags }
Write-Host "
Risky configurations found: $($Risky.Count)/$($Report.Count)" -ForegroundColor Red
$Risky | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  $($_.Name): $($_.RiskyFlags)" -ForegroundColor Red }

Scripts.TaskName: Zero Trust Network Access Readiness Assessment
Scripts.Description: Assesses the environment against Zero Trust principles, checking MFA, device compliance, privileged access, and network segmentation.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================ # Zero Trust Readiness Assessment # ============================================================ Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Policy.Read.All','User.Read.All','DeviceManagementManagedDevices.Read.All' -NoWelcome Import-Module ActiveDirectory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue $OutputPath = "C:\Reports\ZeroTrustAssessment_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').html" $Scores = @{} Write-Host 'Zero Trust Readiness Assessment' -ForegroundColor Cyan Write-Host ('=' * 50) # --- Check 1: MFA Coverage --- Write-Host '[1] Checking MFA coverage...' -ForegroundColor Yellow $Users = Get-MgUser -All -Property Id, AccountEnabled | Where-Object AccountEnabled $MFACount = 0 foreach ($U in $Users | Select-Object -First 100) { # Sample first 100 for speed $Methods = Get-MgUserAuthenticationMethod -UserId $U.Id if ($Methods.Count -gt 1) { $MFACount++ } } $MFAPct = [math]::Round($MFACount / [math]::Min($Users.Count, 100) * 100) $Scores['MFA_Coverage'] = $MFAPct Write-Host " MFA Coverage: $MFAPct%" -ForegroundColor $(if ($MFAPct -ge 95) {'Green'} elseif ($MFAPct -ge 80) {'Yellow'} else {'Red'}) # --- Check 2: Conditional Access Policies --- Write-Host '[2] Checking Conditional Access...' -ForegroundColor Yellow $CAPolicies = Get-MgIdentityConditionalAccessPolicy -All $EnabledCA = ($CAPolicies | Where-Object State -eq 'enabled').Count $MFAPolicy = $CAPolicies | Where-Object { $_.State -eq 'enabled' -and $_.GrantControls.BuiltInControls -contains 'mfa' } $Scores['ConditionalAccess'] = [math]::Min(100, $EnabledCA * 10) Write-Host " CA Policies: $EnabledCA enabled | MFA required by: $($MFAPolicy.Count) policies" -ForegroundColor $(if ($EnabledCA -ge 5) {'Green'} else {'Yellow'}) # --- Check 3: Privileged Account MFA --- Write-Host '[3] Checking privileged account protection...' -ForegroundColor Yellow try { $Admins = Get-ADGroupMember 'Domain Admins' -ErrorAction Stop $AdminMFA = 0 foreach ($Admin in $Admins) { $Methods = Get-MgUserAuthenticationMethod -UserId $Admin.UserPrincipalName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue if ($Methods.Count -gt 1) { $AdminMFA++ } } $AdminMFAPct = if ($Admins.Count -gt 0) { [math]::Round($AdminMFA/$Admins.Count*100) } else { 100 } $Scores['PrivilegedMFA'] = $AdminMFAPct Write-Host " Admin MFA: $AdminMFAPct% ($AdminMFA/$($Admins.Count) admins)" -ForegroundColor $(if ($AdminMFAPct -eq 100) {'Green'} else {'Red'}) } catch { Write-Host ' [SKIP] AD not available' -ForegroundColor Yellow $Scores['PrivilegedMFA'] = 0 } # --- Check 4: Device Compliance --- Write-Host '[4] Checking device compliance...' -ForegroundColor Yellow try { $Devices = Get-MgDeviceManagementManagedDevice -All $Compliant = ($Devices | Where-Object ComplianceState -eq 'compliant').Count $CompliPct = if ($Devices.Count -gt 0) { [math]::Round($Compliant/$Devices.Count*100) } else { 0 } $Scores['DeviceCompliance'] = $CompliPct Write-Host " Device Compliance: $CompliPct% ($Compliant/$($Devices.Count))" -ForegroundColor $(if ($CompliPct -ge 90) {'Green'} elseif ($CompliPct -ge 70) {'Yellow'} else {'Red'}) } catch { $Scores['DeviceCompliance'] = 0 Write-Host ' [SKIP] Intune data unavailable' -ForegroundColor Yellow } # --- Overall Score --- $OverallScore = [math]::Round(($Scores.Values | Measure-Object -Average).Average) $ScoreColor = if ($OverallScore -ge 80) { 'green' } elseif ($OverallScore -ge 60) { 'orange' } else { 'red' } Write-Host " ====================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan Write-Host " ZERO TRUST SCORE: $OverallScore / 100" -ForegroundColor $(if ($OverallScore -ge 80) {'Green'} elseif ($OverallScore -ge 60) {'Yellow'} else {'Red'}) Write-Host "====================================" # Generate HTML report $ScoreRows = $Scores.GetEnumerator() | ForEach-Object { $c = if ($_.Value -ge 80) {'#4CAF50'} elseif ($_.Value -ge 60) {'#FF9800'} else {'#F44336'} "$($_.Key)$($_.Value)%" } | Out-String $HTML = "Zero Trust Assessment

Zero Trust Readiness Assessment

$OverallScore/100
$ScoreRows
CheckScore

Generated: $(Get-Date -f 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm')

" $HTML | Out-File $OutputPath -Encoding UTF8 Write-Host "HTML Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green Start-Process $OutputPath

Discovers SQL Server instances, databases, sizes, recovery models, backup status, and login auditing.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# SQL Server Inventory & Database Report
# ============================================================
# Requires: SqlServer module
# Install-Module SqlServer -Scope CurrentUser
# ============================================================

$SQLServers = @('SQLSERVER01\MSSQLSERVER', 'SQLSERVER02')
$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\SQLInventory_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
$Results    = @()

foreach ($Server in $SQLServers) {
    Write-Host "Connecting to: $Server" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    try {
        $Conn   = New-Object Microsoft.SqlServer.Management.Smo.Server($Server)
        $Version = $Conn.VersionString
        $Edition = $Conn.Edition
        Write-Host "  SQL Server $Version ($Edition)" -ForegroundColor Green

        foreach ($DB in $Conn.Databases) {
            if ($DB.IsSystemObject) { continue }

            # Get last backup info
            $LastFullBackup    = $DB.LastBackupDate
            $LastLogBackup     = $DB.LastLogBackupDate
            $LastDiffBackup    = $DB.LastDifferentialBackupDate
            $DaysSinceBackup   = if ($LastFullBackup -ne [DateTime]::MinValue) {
                [math]::Round(((Get-Date) - $LastFullBackup).TotalDays, 1)
            } else { 'NEVER' }

            $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                Server           = $Server
                SQLVersion       = $Version
                SQLEdition       = $Edition
                DatabaseName     = $DB.Name
                Status           = $DB.Status
                RecoveryModel    = $DB.RecoveryModel
                SizeMB           = [math]::Round($DB.Size, 0)
                SpaceAvailMB     = [math]::Round($DB.SpaceAvailable/1KB, 0)
                Owner            = $DB.Owner
                Created          = $DB.CreateDate
                LastFullBackup   = $LastFullBackup
                LastLogBackup    = $LastLogBackup
                DaysSinceBackup  = $DaysSinceBackup
                CompatibilityLvl = $DB.CompatibilityLevel
                Collation        = $DB.Collation
                IsReadOnly       = $DB.ReadOnly
                AutoShrink       = $DB.AutoShrink
            }

            # Flag no backup
            if ($DaysSinceBackup -eq 'NEVER' -or [int]$DaysSinceBackup -gt 1) {
                Write-Host "  [WARN] $($DB.Name) - Last backup: $DaysSinceBackup days" -ForegroundColor Red
            }
        }
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  [ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    }
}

$Results | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
$TotalSizeGB = [math]::Round(($Results | Measure-Object SizeMB -Sum).Sum/1024, 2)
Write-Host "
Databases: $($Results.Count) | Total Size: ${TotalSizeGB}GB | Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

Scripts.Description: Reports all Hyper-V virtual machines with their state, resource allocation, checkpoints, and replication status.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Hyper-V VM Inventory & Health Report
# ============================================================
$HyperVHosts = @($env:COMPUTERNAME)
$OutputPath  = "C:\Reports\HyperVInventory_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
$Results     = @()

foreach ($Host in $HyperVHosts) {
    Write-Host "Scanning Hyper-V host: $Host" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    try {
        $VMs = Get-VM -ComputerName $Host
        Write-Host "  Found $($VMs.Count) VMs" -ForegroundColor Yellow

        foreach ($VM in $VMs) {
            $VHDs = Get-VMHardDiskDrive -VMName $VM.Name -ComputerName $Host
            $NICs = Get-VMNetworkAdapter -VMName $VM.Name -ComputerName $Host
            $Checkpoints = Get-VMCheckpoint -VMName $VM.Name -ComputerName $Host
            $Replication = Get-VMReplication -VMName $VM.Name -ComputerName $Host -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

            $TotalDiskGB = ($VHDs | ForEach-Object {
                try { (Get-VHD -Path $_.Path -ComputerName $Host).Size / 1GB } catch { 0 }
            } | Measure-Object -Sum).Sum

            $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
                Host              = $Host
                VMName            = $VM.Name
                State             = $VM.State
                Generation        = $VM.Generation
                RAM_GB            = [math]::Round($VM.MemoryAssigned/1GB, 2)
                RAM_Startup_GB    = [math]::Round($VM.MemoryStartup/1GB, 2)
                DynamicMemory     = $VM.DynamicMemoryEnabled
                vCPU              = $VM.ProcessorCount
                DiskCount         = $VHDs.Count
                TotalDisk_GB      = [math]::Round($TotalDiskGB, 2)
                NetworkAdapters   = $NICs.Count
                IPAddresses       = ($NICs.IPAddresses | Where-Object { $_ -notlike '*:*' }) -join '; '
                CheckpointCount   = $Checkpoints.Count
                OldestCheckpoint  = ($Checkpoints | Sort-Object CreationTime | Select-Object -First 1).CreationTime
                ReplicationEnabled = $null -ne $Replication
                ReplicationState  = $Replication.ReplicationState
                Uptime            = $VM.Uptime
                CreatedDate       = $VM.CreationTime
            }

            $Color = if ($VM.State -eq 'Running') { 'Green' } elseif ($VM.State -eq 'Saved') { 'Yellow' } else { 'Red' }
            Write-Host "  $($VM.State) | $($VM.Name) | $([math]::Round($VM.MemoryAssigned/1GB,1))GB RAM | $($VM.ProcessorCount) vCPU" -ForegroundColor $Color
        }
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  [ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    }
}

$Results | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
$Running = ($Results | Where-Object State -eq 'Running').Count
Write-Host "
VMs: $($Results.Count) | Running: $Running | Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

Scripts.Description: Scans servers for expiring SSL/TLS certificates in the Windows certificate store and optionally on public HTTPS endpoints.
Reports backup history for all SQL databases, highlighting missed backups, backup sizes, and duration.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# SQL Server Database Backup Status Report
# ============================================================
# Requires: SqlServer module or access to SQL Server
# ============================================================

$SQLServer  = 'SQLSERVER01'
$DaysBack   = 7
$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\SQLBackupStatus_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"

$Query = @"
SELECT 
    d.name AS DatabaseName,
    d.recovery_model_desc AS RecoveryModel,
    d.state_desc AS DBState,
    bs.type AS BackupType,
    bs.backup_start_date AS BackupStart,
    bs.backup_finish_date AS BackupFinish,
    DATEDIFF(MINUTE, bs.backup_start_date, bs.backup_finish_date) AS DurationMin,
    CAST(bs.backup_size / 1048576.0 AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS BackupSizeMB,
    CAST(bs.compressed_backup_size / 1048576.0 AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS CompressedSizeMB,
    bs.machine_name AS BackupServer,
    bmf.physical_device_name AS BackupFile,
    DATEDIFF(DAY, bs.backup_start_date, GETDATE()) AS DaysAgo
FROM sys.databases d
LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON d.name = bs.database_name
    AND bs.backup_start_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -$DaysBack, GETDATE())
LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily bmf ON bs.media_set_id = bmf.media_set_id
WHERE d.database_id > 4
ORDER BY d.name, bs.backup_start_date DESC
"@

Write-Host "Querying backup history from: $SQLServer" -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {
    $Results = Invoke-Sqlcmd -ServerInstance $SQLServer -Query $Query -TrustServerCertificate
    $Results | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation

    # Find databases with no recent backup
    $DBsWithBackup = ($Results | Where-Object BackupStart | Select-Object -ExpandProperty DatabaseName -Unique)
    $AllDBs = Invoke-Sqlcmd -ServerInstance $SQLServer -Query "SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4" -TrustServerCertificate
    $NoBackup = $AllDBs.name | Where-Object { $_ -notin $DBsWithBackup }

    Write-Host "
Backup Summary (last $DaysBack days):" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $Results | Where-Object BackupStart | Group-Object DatabaseName | ForEach-Object {
        $Latest = $_.Group | Sort-Object BackupStart -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
        Write-Host "  $($_.Name): $($_.Count) backups | Latest: $($Latest.BackupStart) | Size: $($Latest.BackupSizeMB)MB"
    }

    if ($NoBackup) {
        Write-Host "
[WARN] Databases with NO recent backup:" -ForegroundColor Red
        $NoBackup | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "  - $_" -ForegroundColor Red }
    }

    Write-Host "
Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green
} catch {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host 'Ensure SqlServer module is installed: Install-Module SqlServer' -ForegroundColor Yellow
}

# Summarize by user
$UserSummary = $Activity | Group-Object 'User Principal Name' | ForEach-Object {
    [PSCustomObject]@{
        UserUPN         = $_.Name
        TotalActivities = $_.Count
        ReportsViewed   = ($_.Group | Where-Object 'Activity Type' -eq 'ViewReport').Count
        DashboardViews  = ($_.Group | Where-Object 'Activity Type' -eq 'ViewDashboard').Count
        DatasetRefresh  = ($_.Group | Where-Object 'Activity Type' -like '*Refresh*').Count
        LastActivity    = ($_.Group.'Activity Date/Time' | Sort-Object -Descending | Select-Object -First 1)
    }
} | Sort-Object TotalActivities -Descending

$UserSummary | Export-Csv "$OutputDir\UserSummary.csv" -NoTypeInformation

# Artifact Report
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/reports/getPowerBIReportUsageUserDetail(period=D30)' `
    -OutputFilePath "$OutputDir\ReportUsage.csv"

Write-Host "
Top 10 active Power BI users:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
$UserSummary | Select-Object -First 10 | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "  $($_.UserUPN) - $($_.TotalActivities) activities (Reports: $($_.ReportsViewed))"
}
Write-Host "
Reports saved to: $OutputDir" -ForegroundColor Green

Scripts.TaskName: Copilot for M365 License & Usage Report
Scripts.Description: Reports Microsoft 365 Copilot license assignments, active users, and feature adoption rates via Graph API.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Microsoft 365 Copilot License & Usage Report
# ============================================================
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Reports.Read.All','User.Read.All','Organization.Read.All' -NoWelcome

$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\CopilotUsage_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"

Write-Host 'Fetching M365 Copilot license data...' -ForegroundColor Cyan

# Get Copilot licenses (Microsoft_365_Copilot SKU)
$SKUs        = Get-MgSubscribedSku
$CopilotSKU  = $SKUs | Where-Object { $_.SkuPartNumber -like '*Copilot*' -or $_.ServicePlans.ServicePlanName -like '*Copilot*' }

if ($CopilotSKU) {
    Write-Host "Copilot SKU: $($CopilotSKU.SkuPartNumber)" -ForegroundColor Green
    Write-Host "  Purchased: $($CopilotSKU.PrepaidUnits.Enabled) | Assigned: $($CopilotSKU.ConsumedUnits) | Available: $($CopilotSKU.PrepaidUnits.Enabled - $CopilotSKU.ConsumedUnits)"
} else {
    Write-Host '[WARN] No Copilot SKU found in tenant' -ForegroundColor Yellow
}

# Get users with Copilot license
Write-Host 'Finding licensed users...' -ForegroundColor Cyan
$LicensedUsers = Get-MgUser -All -Property Id, DisplayName, UserPrincipalName, Department, AssignedLicenses |
    Where-Object { $_.AssignedLicenses.SkuId -contains $CopilotSKU.SkuId }

Write-Host "Users with Copilot license: $($LicensedUsers.Count)" -ForegroundColor Yellow

# Get Copilot usage report
try {
    $ReportPath = "$env:TEMP\CopilotUserDetail.csv"
    Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
        -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/reports/getMicrosoft365CopilotUsageUserDetail(period=D30)' `
        -OutputFilePath $ReportPath

    $UsageData = Import-Csv $ReportPath

    $Report = $LicensedUsers | ForEach-Object {
        $User  = $_
        $Usage = $UsageData | Where-Object { $_.'User Principal Name' -eq $User.UserPrincipalName }
        [PSCustomObject]@{
            UPN             = $User.UserPrincipalName
            DisplayName     = $User.DisplayName
            Department      = $User.Department
            Licensed        = $true
            LastActivity    = $Usage.'Last Activity Date'
            TeamsActivity   = $Usage.'Copilot In Teams Chat Last Activity Date'
            WordActivity    = $Usage.'Copilot In Word Last Activity Date'
            OutlookActivity = $Usage.'Copilot In Outlook Last Activity Date'
            ExcelActivity   = $Usage.'Copilot In Excel Last Activity Date'
            PPTActivity     = $Usage.'Copilot In PowerPoint Last Activity Date'
            IsActive        = $null -ne $Usage.'Last Activity Date' -and $Usage.'Last Activity Date' -ne ''
        }
    }

    $Report | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
    $Active   = ($Report | Where-Object IsActive).Count
    $Inactive = ($Report | Where-Object { -not $_.IsActive }).Count
    $AdoptPct = if ($Report.Count -gt 0) { [math]::Round($Active/$Report.Count*100, 1) } else { 0 }

    Write-Host "
Copilot Adoption: $AdoptPct% ($Active active / $($Report.Count) licensed)"
    Write-Host "Inactive (potential savings): $Inactive users"
    Write-Host "Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

} catch {
    Write-Host "[WARN] Usage data unavailable: $_" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $LicensedUsers | Select-Object DisplayName, UserPrincipalName, Department | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
    Write-Host "License list exported to: $OutputPath"
}

Scripts.Description: Monitors Azure AI and Cognitive Services quotas, usage metrics, and costs across all AI resources in the subscription.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Azure AI Service Quota & Cost Monitor
# ============================================================
Connect-AzAccount

$SubscriptionId = 'YOUR-SUBSCRIPTION-ID'
$OutputPath     = "C:\Reports\AIQuotaCosts_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"
Set-AzContext -SubscriptionId $SubscriptionId

Write-Host 'Gathering Azure AI / Cognitive Services resources...' -ForegroundColor Cyan

$AIResources = Get-AzResource -ResourceType 'Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts'
Write-Host "Found $($AIResources.Count) AI/Cognitive resources" -ForegroundColor Yellow

$Results = @()
foreach ($Resource in $AIResources) {
    try {
        $Details = Get-AzCognitiveServicesAccount `
            -ResourceGroupName $Resource.ResourceGroupName `
            -Name $Resource.Name

        # Get metrics via Az Monitor
        $MetricParams = @{
            ResourceId  = $Resource.ResourceId
            MetricName  = 'TotalCalls'
            StartTime   = (Get-Date).AddDays(-30)
            EndTime     = Get-Date
            AggregationType = 'Total'
            TimeGrain   = 'P1D'
        }
        $Metrics = Get-AzMetric @MetricParams -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        $TotalCalls = ($Metrics.Data | Measure-Object Total -Sum).Sum

        # Errors
        $ErrMetric = Get-AzMetric -ResourceId $Resource.ResourceId -MetricName 'TotalErrors' `
            -StartTime (Get-Date).AddDays(-7) -EndTime (Get-Date) -AggregationType Total `
            -TimeGrain PT1H -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        $TotalErrors = ($ErrMetric.Data | Measure-Object Total -Sum).Sum

        $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
            ResourceName      = $Resource.Name
            ResourceGroup     = $Resource.ResourceGroupName
            Kind              = $Details.Kind
            SKU               = $Details.Sku.Name
            Location          = $Details.Location
            Endpoint          = $Details.Properties.Endpoint
            ProvisioningState = $Details.Properties.ProvisioningState
            PublicNetAccess   = $Details.Properties.PublicNetworkAccess
            TotalCalls_30d    = $TotalCalls
            TotalErrors_7d    = $TotalErrors
            ErrorRate_Pct     = if ($TotalCalls -gt 0) { [math]::Round($TotalErrors/$TotalCalls*100,2) } else { 0 }
            Tags              = ($Resource.Tags.GetEnumerator() | ForEach-Object { "$($_.Key)=$($_.Value)" }) -join '; '
        }

        Write-Host "  $($Resource.Name) ($($Details.Kind)) - Calls: $TotalCalls | Errors: $TotalErrors" -ForegroundColor Green
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  [ERROR] $($Resource.Name): $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    }
}

$Results | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "
AI resources audited: $($Results.Count) | Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green

Scripts.TaskName: Azure AI Content Safety Policy Audit
Scripts.Description: Reviews Azure AI Content Safety resource configurations and tests content filtering policies for text and image moderation.
Scripts.PowerShellCode:
# ============================================================
# Azure AI Content Safety Policy Audit
# ============================================================
Connect-AzAccount

$SubscriptionId = 'YOUR-SUBSCRIPTION-ID'
Set-AzContext -SubscriptionId $SubscriptionId

$OutputPath = "C:\Reports\ContentSafety_$(Get-Date -f 'yyyyMMdd').csv"

Write-Host 'Auditing Azure AI Content Safety resources...' -ForegroundColor Cyan

# Find Content Safety resources
$CSResources = Get-AzResource -ResourceType 'Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts' |
               Where-Object { $_.Kind -eq 'ContentSafety' }

Write-Host "Found $($CSResources.Count) Content Safety resources" -ForegroundColor Yellow

$Token   = (Get-AzAccessToken).Token
$Headers = @{ 'Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key' = ''; Authorization = "Bearer $Token" }
$Results = @()

foreach ($Resource in $CSResources) {
    $Details = Get-AzCognitiveServicesAccount `
        -ResourceGroupName $Resource.ResourceGroupName `
        -Name $Resource.Name

    $Endpoint = $Details.Properties.Endpoint

    # Get blocklists
    try {
        $BlocklistsResp = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "${Endpoint}contentsafety/text/blocklists?api-version=2023-10-01" `
            -Method GET -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $Token" }
        $Blocklists = $BlocklistsResp.value
    } catch { $Blocklists = @() }

    $Results += [PSCustomObject]@{
        ResourceName      = $Resource.Name
        ResourceGroup     = $Resource.ResourceGroupName
        Location          = $Details.Location
        SKU               = $Details.Sku.Name
        Endpoint          = $Endpoint
        ProvisioningState = $Details.Properties.ProvisioningState
        PublicAccess      = $Details.Properties.PublicNetworkAccess
        BlocklistCount    = $Blocklists.Count
        Blocklists        = ($Blocklists.blocklistName) -join '; '
        ManagedIdentity   = $Details.Identity.Type
        Tags              = ($Resource.Tags.GetEnumerator() | ForEach-Object { "$($_.Key)=$($_.Value)" }) -join '; '
    }

    Write-Host "  $($Resource.Name) | Blocklists: $($Blocklists.Count) | Public: $($Details.Properties.PublicNetworkAccess)" -ForegroundColor $(if ($Details.Properties.PublicNetworkAccess -eq 'Enabled') { 'Yellow' } else { 'Green' })
}

$Results | Export-Csv $OutputPath -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "
Audit complete: $($Results.Count) resources | Report: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Green
